---
title: "A quartobot-manuscript example"
subtitle: "What this template gives you"
author:
  - name: Your Name
    affiliation: Your Institution
    orcid: 0000-0000-0000-0000
    corresponding: true
    email: you@example.org
date: today
abstract: |
  This is the template's example manuscript. Replace the prose and
  metadata with your own and start writing. Citations work by persistent
  identifier — paste a DOI, PubMed ID, arXiv ID, or ISBN into your text
  and the bibliography updates on next render.
keywords:
  - quartobot
  - manubot
  - reproducible-publishing
---

# Introduction

This template combines [Quarto](https://quarto.org), the
[`quarto-manubot-cite`](https://github.com/seandavi/quartobot) extension,
and a CI workflow that gives every commit an immutable permalink at
`/v/<sha>/`, deploys HTML and PDF to GitHub Pages, and posts PR previews
through a sticky comment. The pattern is manubot's
[@doi:10.1371/journal.pcbi.1007128], lifted onto Quarto's publishing
stack [@quarto2024].

You're reading the rendered version. The source lives in
`index.qmd` next to this file. Edit the prose, push, and CI takes care of
the rest.

# What's wired up

Two things to notice in the bibliography below:

- `@doi:10.1371/journal.pcbi.1007128` resolved automatically. You wrote
  the DOI in the prose; `pandoc-manubot-cite` fetched the metadata from
  Crossref at render time and wrote it to `references.json`. The cache
  in `_freeze/manubot-cache.json` means subsequent renders stay offline.
- `@quarto2024` is a hand-curated entry from `references.bib`. Use this
  form when you want full control over the bibliography record — citing
  software, grey literature, or anything the resolver doesn't have good
  metadata for.

Both files are declared under `bibliography:` in `_quarto.yml` and pandoc
citeproc merges them at render.

# Citation key vocabulary

Manubot's prefix syntax, available as-is:

| Prefix      | Example                                |
|-------------|----------------------------------------|
| `@doi:`     | `@doi:10.1371/journal.pcbi.1007128`    |
| `@pmid:`    | `@pmid:31479462`                       |
| `@pmcid:`   | `@pmcid:PMC6735409`                    |
| `@arxiv:`   | `@arxiv:2104.10729`                    |
| `@isbn:`    | `@isbn:9780262035613`                  |
| `@url:`     | `@url:https://manubot.org`             |
| `@wikidata:`| `@wikidata:Q56458094`                  |
| bare DOI    | `@10.1371/journal.pcbi.1007128`        |

# Next steps

- Edit author metadata in the YAML header.
- Replace this prose with your own.
- Add citations as you go — by DOI/PMID where possible, hand-curated for
  the rest.
- Push to `main`. CI renders HTML, PDF, and DOCX; deploys to
  `https://<owner>.github.io/<repo>/`; embeds the per-commit permalink
  in the HTML version banner.
- Open a PR. CI posts a sticky comment with the preview URL.

The full design is in [DESIGN.md](https://github.com/seandavi/quartobot/blob/main/DESIGN.md);
the extension's behavior is documented at
[`_extensions/seandavi/quarto-manubot-cite/`](https://github.com/seandavi/quartobot/blob/main/_extensions/seandavi/quarto-manubot-cite/README.md).

# References {.unnumbered}

::: {#refs}
:::
